title: 2024年度 第1回（準会場）英検2級 リーディング

vocabulary.example: Mandy and her sister Jessica greatly resemble each other.
Many of the bridges are made of stone or brick.
Victoria tries to feed her dog many different kinds of foods.
Blake's first instinct was to run when he saw a large bear.
Patrick was obliged to wait outside until his mother came back.
Running a marathon is not only hard on athletes' bodies but also mentally challenging.
Drinking alcohol goes against his religious beliefs.
She decided to make an adjustment to the schedule.
The company realized the need to adapt to the changing demands.
Darcy was so nervous that her hands began to tremble.
Jessica Lawson's latest novel is a story of hope.
Henry is not allowed to walk to the store alone.
Lunch is on me today.
This calls for a celebration!
Many animal species will be wiped out.
I look more like my dad, but I behave more like my mom.
I just need to arrange for someone to take care of my dog.
One kind of unusual pet is a tortoise.
Tortoises spend the winter months sleeping.
Skunks are small animals with black and white fur.
Skunks produce a liquid with a bad smell to protect themselves.
Wallabies come from Australia and are similar to kangaroos.
Chemicals are often added to keep the soil healthy.
Green grass needs chemicals and nitrogen to grow well.
Environmental concerns about using chemicals have made grass paint popular.
In the past, grass paint did not look natural and it faded quickly.
Products made from natural ingredients that are not harmful.
Construction was carried out by people of the Maya civilization.
They designed the Temple of Kukulcán in Chichén Itzá.
The temple was designed to work as a calendar.
A shadow on the temple appears in the shape of a snake's body.
The echo sounds like the call of a bird called a quetzal.
The Maya people believed these birds were the messengers of the gods.
Chichén Itzá is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Chichén Itzá is a popular destination for tourists.
Chichén Itzá contains some remarkable buildings.
The site had a large underground source of water.
Large groups can be challenging and inconvenient for guides.
I suggest that you divide your students into five groups.
Some workers use public transportation such as buses or trains.
Others ride bicycles for exercise because they don't have time during the day.
Some roads are too narrow for people to ride bicycles.
They have to feel uncomfortable in wet clothes after arriving at work.
Knowing dates would help local farmers to know when to plant their crops.
European explorers first came to Chichén Itzá in the 16th century.
The buildings have been closed due to the damage caused by visitors.
Protect these wonderful structures for many years to come.
The grass can survive cold winters and hot summers.
It was mostly used to maintain sports fields and golf courses.
Many US homeowners love having a beautiful yard.
Some people prefer to keep unusual pets.

sections.name: 大問1
sections.instruction: 次の(1)から(17)までの(　)に入れるのに最も適切なものを1，2，3，4の中から一つ選びなさい。

questions.text: Mandy and her sister Jessica greatly (　) each other, and many people mistake them for twins. However, Mandy was born two years before Jessica.
questions.translation: マンディと姉のジェシカはとても( 1 )で、多くの人が双子と間違える。しかしマンディはジェシカの2年前に生まれた。
choiceAnalysis: resemble=似ている→正解。💡 mistake them for twins=双子と間違えるほど似ている
expand=拡大する→不適切
convince=説得する→不適切
organize=整理する→不適切

questions.text: In the old part of the city, many of the bridges are made of stone or (　). All of the newer bridges are made of metal and concrete.
questions.translation: 旧市街では多くの橋が石や( 2 )で作られている。新しい橋はすべて金属とコンクリート製だ。
choiceAnalysis: blade=刃→不適切
comb=櫛→不適切
affair=事件→不適切
brick=レンガ→正解。💡 stone or brick=石やレンガ(建築材料)

questions.text: Victoria tries to (　) her dog many different kinds of foods. Sometimes, she gives him steak as a special treat.
questions.translation: ビクトリアは犬にさまざまな種類の食べ物を( 3 )しようとする。時にはご褒美としてステーキをあげる。
choiceAnalysis: negotiate=交渉する→不適切
feed=えさを与える→正解。💡 different kinds of foods+steak as treat=食べ物を与える
prove=証明する→不適切
repair=修理する→不適切

questions.text: Blake's first (　) was to run when he saw a large bear by his tent. However, he was able to keep calm and slowly move away from the animal.
questions.translation: テントの近くに大きなクマを見た時、ブレイクの最初の( 4 )は走ることだった。しかし冷静さを保ちゆっくり離れることができた。
choiceAnalysis: connection=つながり→不適切
degree=程度→不適切
instinct=本能→正解。💡 first instinct=最初の本能的反応→走ること
approval=承認→不適切

questions.text: Patrick could not find his front door key, so he was (　) to wait outside his house until his mother came back from work.
questions.translation: パトリックは玄関の鍵が見つからず、母が仕事から戻るまで家の外で待つことを( 5 )された。
choiceAnalysis: sensitive=敏感な→不適切
entitled=資格のある→不適切
obliged=義務づけられた→正解。💡 could not find key→was obliged to wait=待たざるを得なかった
reluctant=気が進まない→不適切

questions.text: Running a marathon is not only hard on athletes' bodies but also (　) challenging. Athletes need to be able to keep going, even when they want to stop and rest.
questions.translation: マラソンは体だけでなく( 6 )にも大変だ。選手は止まりたい時でも走り続けなければならない。
choiceAnalysis: grammatically=文法的に→不適切
evenly=均等に→不適切
mentally=精神的に→正解。💡 bodies+also mentally=体だけでなく精神的にも
vividly=生き生きと→不適切

questions.text: Hakim says that drinking alcohol goes against his religious (　) as a Muslim. According to Islam, people should avoid alcohol because it can cause people to behave in bad ways.
questions.translation: ハキムはアルコールを飲むことはイスラム教徒としての宗教的( 7 )に反すると言う。
choiceAnalysis: assignments=課題→不適切
images=映像→不適切
certificates=証明書→不適切
beliefs=信仰→正解。💡 religious beliefs=宗教的信仰

questions.text: When the boss realized that the sales team could not finish the project in time, she decided to make an (　) to the schedule. She gave the team one more week to complete it.
questions.translation: 上司はチームが期限内にプロジェクトを終えられないと気づき、スケジュールの( 8 )を決めた。チームにあと1週間を与えた。
choiceAnalysis: adjustment=調整→正解。💡 make an adjustment to the schedule=スケジュール調整+one more week
engagement=婚約→不適切
interruption=中断→不適切
occupation=職業→不適切

questions.text: The company realized the need to (　) to the changing demands of consumers. As a result, they introduced a new marketing plan.
questions.translation: 会社は消費者の変化する需要に( 9 )する必要性を認識した。結果、新しいマーケティング計画を導入した。
choiceAnalysis: adapt=適応する→正解。💡 adapt to changing demands=変化する需要に適応する
insist=主張する→不適切
regret=後悔する→不適切
permit=許可する→不適切

questions.text: Darcy was so nervous about playing the flute at the concert that her hands began to (　). At one point, she almost dropped her instrument.
questions.translation: ダーシーはコンサートでフルートを演奏するのがとても緊張して手が( 10 )始めた。ある時、楽器を落としそうになった。
choiceAnalysis: tremble=震える→正解。💡 nervous+almost dropped=緊張で手が震えた
dissolve=溶ける→不適切
flourish=繁栄する→不適切
retrieve=取り戻す→不適切

questions.text: Jessica Lawson's latest novel is a story (　) hope. The main character has cancer, but she recovers and lives into old age.
questions.translation: ジェシカ・ローソンの最新小説は希望( 11 )物語だ。主人公はがんだが回復し老年まで生きる。
choiceAnalysis: onto=～の上に→不適切
as=～として→不適切
by=～によって→不適切
of=～の→正解。💡 a story of hope=希望の物語

questions.text: Henry is not (　) walk to the store alone. He has to go with his mother or father.
questions.translation: ヘンリーは一人で店まで歩いて行くことを( 12 )。母か父と一緒に行かなければならない。
choiceAnalysis: allowed to=許されている→正解。💡 not allowed to=許されていない+has to go with parents
intended to=～するつもり→不適切
concerned about=心配している→不適切
pleased about=喜んでいる→不適切

questions.text:
A : Put away your wallet, Dave. Lunch is (　) me today.
B : Thanks, Barbara, but you don't have to do that. I'm happy to pay for myself.
questions.translation:
A：財布をしまって、デイブ。今日のランチは私の( 13 )よ。
B：ありがとう、バーバラ。でもそんなことしなくていいよ。自分で払うよ。
choiceAnalysis: on=おごりで→正解。💡 Lunch is on me=私のおごり。put away wallet=財布をしまって
at→不適切
about→不適切
over→不適切

questions.text:
A : Guess what, Mom! I passed my high school entrance exams!
B : This (　) a celebration. Let's have a party!
questions.translation:
A：聞いて、ママ！高校の入試に受かったよ！
B：これは( 14 )ね。パーティーをしましょう！
choiceAnalysis: calls for=～を必要とする→正解。💡 calls for a celebration=お祝いが必要だ
hangs on=待つ→不適切
leaves out=省く→不適切
bets on=賭ける→不適切

questions.text: If people continue to destroy rainforests, many animal species will be (　) because they can only survive in these environments.
questions.translation: 人々が熱帯雨林を破壊し続ければ、多くの動物種は( 15 )。その環境でしか生存できないからだ。
choiceAnalysis: wiped out=一掃される→正解。💡 destroy rainforests+can only survive=動物が絶滅する
blown down=吹き倒される→不適切
held on=持ちこたえる→不適切
turned off=消される→不適切

questions.text:
A : Who do you (　), your mom or your dad?
B : Both, I guess. I look more like my dad, but I behave more like my mom.
questions.translation:
A：お母さんとお父さん、どっちに( 16 )？
B：両方かな。見た目はパパ似だけど行動はママ似。
choiceAnalysis: give out=配る→不適切
fill in=記入する→不適切
make over=改造する→不適切
take after=～に似る→正解。💡 look like dad+behave like mom=両親に似る

questions.text:
A : Are you ready for your trip to Hawaii?
B : I just need to (　) someone to take care of my dog. I think one of my friends might be able to do it.
questions.translation:
A：ハワイ旅行の準備はできた？
B：犬の世話をしてくれる人を( 17 )するだけ。友達の一人ができるかも。
choiceAnalysis: finish off=終える→不適切
arrange for=手配する→正解。💡 arrange for someone to take care=世話する人を手配する
hand in=提出する→不適切
wear out=すり減る→不適切

sections.name: 大問2
sections.instruction: 次の英文A，Bを読み，その文意にそって(18)から(23)までの(　)に入れるのに最も適切なものを選びなさい。

passages.label: A
passages.title: Unusual Pets
paragraphs: While most pet owners choose traditional pets such as dogs and cats, some people prefer to keep unusual pets. One kind of unusual pet is a tortoise. Tortoises look similar to turtles. However, tortoises live on land. Tortoises are easy to take care of because they move slowly and only need to eat things like leaves and flowers. Furthermore, they spend the winter months sleeping. They become active again when the weather becomes warmer. ( 18 ), tortoise owners only need to check on their pets once a day.

Skunks are small animals with black and white fur that are sometimes kept as pets. Wild skunks are famous for producing a liquid with a bad smell to protect themselves from attackers. If a skunk is to be kept as a pet, the part of its body that makes this liquid is removed by an animal doctor. Skunks can be fun pets because they are clever and they enjoy playing. However, they also ( 19 ). In the wild, skunks use these to dig holes in the ground. Inside a house, they can use them to open doors and refrigerators and cause trouble for their owners.

Another unusual pet is a wallaby. Wallabies come from Australia. They are similar to kangaroos but smaller. Just like kangaroos, wallabies stand on their back legs and use their front legs to ( 20 ). While they are young, they do this for fun, and this behavior can seem cute. However, if pet wallabies are not trained to stop doing this at an early age, they can end up hurting their owners.
translations: ほとんどのペット飼い主は犬や猫などの伝統的なペットを選ぶが、珍しいペットを飼うことを好む人もいる。珍しいペットの一つはリクガメだ。リクガメはカメに似ているが陸上で生活する。ゆっくり動き、葉や花を食べるだけでよいので世話が楽だ。さらに冬は冬眠する。暖かくなると再び活動的になる。( 18 )、リクガメの飼い主は1日1回ペットを確認するだけでよい。
スカンクは黒と白の毛の小さな動物でペットとして飼われることもある。野生のスカンクは攻撃者から身を守るために悪臭のする液体を出すことで有名だ。ペットとして飼う場合、その液体を出す体の部分が獣医によって除去される。スカンクは賢くて遊ぶのが好きなので楽しいペットになれる。しかし彼らは( 19 )もある。野生ではこれを使って地面に穴を掘る。家の中ではドアや冷蔵庫を開けて飼い主を困らせることがある。
ワラビーはオーストラリア原産でカンガルーに似ているが小さい。カンガルーと同様に後ろ足で立ち、前足を使って( 20 )。子供のうちは遊びでこれをするのでかわいく見える。しかしペットのワラビーが幼いうちにこれをやめさせる訓練をしないと、飼い主を傷つけることになりかねない。
sentencePairs: Some people prefer to keep unusual pets.
珍しいペットを飼うことを好む人もいる。
Tortoises are easy to take care of.
リクガメは世話が楽だ。
Wild skunks produce a liquid with a bad smell.
野生のスカンクは悪臭のする液体を出す。
They can use their nails to open doors and refrigerators.
爪を使ってドアや冷蔵庫を開けることができる。
Wallabies stand on their back legs and use their front legs.
ワラビーは後ろ足で立ち前足を使う。
If pet wallabies are not trained, they can end up hurting their owners.
訓練しないとワラビーは飼い主を傷つけることがある。

choiceAnalysis: 同様に→前文と同列でない
それまでは→正解。💡 冬眠→暖かくなると活動→Until then(それまでは)世話が少ない→1日1回でOK
それにもかかわらず→論理が逆
いつものように→不適切
長い爪がある→正解。💡 use these to dig holes+open doors=爪で穴を掘り、ドアを開ける
虫やクモを食べる→不適切
大きな音を出す→不適切
登るのが好き→不適切
体をきれいにする→不適切
木から果物を取る→不適切
頭を掻く→不適切
ボクサーのように戦う→正解。💡 前足で打つ動作→かわいいが訓練しないと飼い主を傷つける

passages.label: B
passages.title: Greener Grass?
paragraphs: Many US homeowners love having a beautiful yard, but having a yard covered in thick, green grass takes money and time. Chemicals are often added to keep the soil healthy so that the grass can survive cold winters and hot summers. Lately, however, more homeowners have been ( 21 ). Instead of trying to help the grass grow, they use a special paint to color it green. This creates the look of a healthy yard.

Grass paint has actually been available for over fifty years. At that time, it was mostly used to maintain sports fields and golf courses. Nowadays, ( 22 ) and environmental concerns about using chemicals have made it a popular choice for people. This is especially true in areas where the climate is becoming drier. In these areas, cities sometimes limit water usage when water supplies are low. This lack of water can turn yards brown, but grass paint can help hide this.

While painting a yard can make it look better, it takes a lot of work. The grass needs to be cut, and the yard needs to be painted again. Still, things have come a long way. In the past, grass paint was hard to use because it did not stick to the grass well. The color did not look natural, and it faded quickly. ( 23 ), a number of improvements were made. Today, homeowners have a variety of choices, including products made from natural ingredients that are not harmful to yards. Just like grass, it seems this trend will continue to grow.
translations: アメリカの住宅所有者の多くは美しい庭を持つことを好むが、青々とした厚い芝の庭はお金と時間がかかる。冬の寒さや夏の暑さに芝が耐えられるよう化学物質がよく追加される。しかし最近、多くの住宅所有者は( 21 )。芝の成長を助ける代わりに特別なペンキを使って緑に着色する。これにより健康な庭の見た目が作られる。
芝ペンキは実際50年以上前から利用可能だった。当時はスポーツ場やゴルフコースの維持に使われていた。現在、( 22 )や化学物質使用に関する環境への懸念により、一般の人にも人気の選択肢となった。特に気候が乾燥化している地域では顕著だ。これらの地域では水の供給が少ない時に水の使用を制限することがあり、庭が茶色くなるのを芝ペンキで隠せる。
庭をペイントすると見栄えは良くなるが、多くの作業が必要だ。芝を刈り、再塗装する必要がある。それでも状況は大きく改善した。以前は芝にうまく付着せず色も自然に見えず、すぐに色あせた。( 23 )、多くの改良が行われた。今日では天然成分から作られた庭に害のない製品など多様な選択肢がある。芝のように、このトレンドは成長し続けるだろう。
sentencePairs: Having a yard covered in thick, green grass takes money and time.
青々とした芝の庭はお金と時間がかかる。
They use a special paint to color the grass green.
特別なペンキで芝を緑に着色する。
Grass paint has been available for over fifty years.
芝ペンキは50年以上前から利用可能だった。
Environmental concerns have made it a popular choice.
環境への懸念が人気の選択肢にした。
In the past, grass paint did not look natural and it faded quickly.
以前は自然に見えずすぐに色あせた。
Today, homeowners have a variety of choices.
今日、多様な選択肢がある。

choiceAnalysis: 野生動物に対処→不適切
芝を取り除く→不適切
別の方法を試す→正解。💡 Instead of trying to help grass grow, use paint=従来の方法ではなく別のアプローチ(ペイント)
地元の法律と闘う→不適切
より良い広告計画→不適切
より多くの購入選択肢→正解。💡 more buying options+environmental concerns=選択肢の増加と環境懸念で人気に
植物コストの増加→不適切
頻繁な雨不足→不適切
基本的に→不適切
原則として→不適切
最終的に→不適切
しかも→正解。💡 前文の問題点→What is more(さらに/しかも)→improvements were made=改良が行われた

sections.name: 大問3
sections.instruction: 次の英文A，Bの内容に関して，(24)から(31)までの質問に対して最も適切なものを選びなさい。

passages.label: A
passages.title: Museum Tours
paragraphs:
Dear Mr. Bowen,
Thank you for your e-mail. I would be very glad to hear that students at your college are coming here to the Wallington History Museum to help with museum tours. During this time of year, the museum gets many visitors, including young children on school trips. Because we only have a few tour guides, they often have to take care of very large groups, and this can be challenging for guides and inconvenient for visitors.

Before your students can give tours, they will need to be trained by our tour guides. The museum has five rooms, and each room covers a different period of history and contains about fifty items. It might be difficult for your students to learn about all the items in all the rooms, so I suggest that you divide your students into five groups, and the members of each group can learn about the items in one of the rooms.

I would like to have a meeting with you soon in order to set up a schedule for your students' training. Either I can go to your college, you can come here, or we can have an online meeting. I am usually busy on weekday mornings, so if you have some time on one afternoon next week, that would be better for me. I look forward to hearing from you soon.
Sincerely,
Barbara Miller
Director
Wallington History Museum
translations: ボウエン先生、メールありがとうございます。あなたの大学の学生がウォリントン歴史博物館に来て博物館ツアーを手伝ってくれると聞いてとても嬉しいです。この時期、博物館には修学旅行の子供を含む多くの来訪者があります。ツアーガイドが数人しかおらず、大人数のグループを担当しなければならないことが多く、ガイドにとっても来訪者にとっても大変です。
学生がツアーを行う前に、当館のツアーガイドによる研修が必要です。博物館には5つの部屋があり、各部屋は異なる時代をカバーし約50のアイテムがあります。すべての部屋の全アイテムを学ぶのは難しいかもしれないので、学生を5つのグループに分け、各グループが1つの部屋のアイテムを学ぶことを提案します。
学生の研修スケジュールを立てるため、近いうちにお会いしたいと思います。私があなたの大学に行くか、あなたがここに来るか、オンライン会議も可能です。平日の午前中は通常忙しいので、来週の午後にお時間があればその方がよいです。
sentencePairs: The museum gets many visitors, including young children on school trips.
博物館には修学旅行の子供を含む多くの来訪者がある。
We only have a few tour guides.
ツアーガイドは数人しかいない。
Each room covers a different period of history.
各部屋は異なる時代をカバーする。
I suggest that you divide your students into five groups.
学生を5つのグループに分けることを提案します。
I am usually busy on weekday mornings.
平日の午前中は通常忙しいです。

questions.question: What is one thing Barbara Miller mentions about tour guides at the museum?
choices: Many of them studied history when they were in college.
They always look forward to showing schoolchildren around.
Many of them take time off work at this time of year.
They often must look after groups with many members.
choiceAnalysis: 大学で歴史を学んだ→本文にない
子供案内を楽しみにしている→本文にない
この時期に休みを取る→本文にない
多人数グループを担当しなければならない→正解。💡 have to take care of very large groups

questions.question: Barbara Miller advises Mr. Bowen to
choices: separate his students into several groups.
teach about a different period of history.
speak to one of the museum's tour guides directly.
choose fifty items for his students to learn about.
choiceAnalysis: 学生を複数のグループに分ける→正解。💡 divide your students into five groups
異なる時代について教える→ミラーの提案ではない
ガイドに直接話す→不適切
50アイテムを選ぶ→不適切

questions.question: Barbara Miller says that she
choices: would like to avoid having an online meeting.
wants to speak with Mr. Bowen to create a plan.
does not have much time on weekday afternoons.
is not sure how to get to Mr. Bowen's college.
choiceAnalysis: オンライン会議を避けたい→オンラインも選択肢に入れている
ボウエン氏と話して計画を作りたい→正解。💡 have a meeting to set up a schedule
平日午後に時間がない→午前が忙しいと言っている
大学への行き方がわからない→不適切

passages.label: B
passages.title: Chichén Itzá
paragraphs: Chichén Itzá is an ancient city in Mexico that contains some remarkable buildings. Construction of the city began around 1,500 years ago and was carried out by people of the Maya civilization. The site was probably first settled by the Maya people because it had a large, underground source of water, good soil for growing crops, and plenty of stone for building. For these reasons, the area was able to support a large population, and within a few hundred years, Chichén Itzá had become one of the most important Maya cities.

Among the people of the Maya civilization, there were great architects, artists, and scientists. They had observed that one year lasted 365 days, and they designed a building called the Temple of Kukulcán in Chichén Itzá to work as a calendar. It is believed that this temple was made so that a shadow on the temple in the shape of a snake's body joined up with statues of a snake's head on certain special days. Knowing such dates would help local farmers to know when to plant their crops.

The Temple of Kukulcán is also special for another reason. If a person stands in front of one of the temple's stairs and claps their hands, the echo sounds like the call of a bird called a quetzal. Quetzals are beautiful birds with bright green, red, and white feathers. The Maya people believed that these birds were the messengers of the gods. Clapping in front of the Temple of Kukulcán sounds as if the gods were talking to the Maya people through their messengers.

Sometime before European explorers first came to Chichén Itzá in the 16th century, the Maya people had left the city. Nobody is sure of the reason for this, but researchers are continuing to look for answers. These days, Chichén Itzá is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a popular destination for tourists. However, the buildings have been closed to the public due to the damage caused by visitors. It is hoped that doing this will protect these wonderful structures for many years to come so that they can be studied and enjoyed.
translations: チチェン・イッツァはメキシコの古代都市で注目すべき建物がある。約1500年前に建設が始まりマヤ文明の人々によって行われた。大きな地下水源、農業に適した土壌、建設用の豊富な石があったため、マヤの人々がここに定住したと考えられる。これらの理由で大人口を支えることができ、数百年のうちにマヤの最も重要な都市の一つとなった。
マヤ文明の人々の中には偉大な建築家、芸術家、科学者がいた。1年が365日であることを観測し、チチェン・イッツァにクルクルカンの神殿という建物を暦として機能するよう設計した。特定の日に蛇の体の形をした影が蛇の頭の彫像とつながるように神殿が作られたと考えられている。このような日付を知ることで地元の農民は作物の植え時を知ることができた。
クルクルカン神殿は別の理由でも特別だ。神殿の階段の前に立って手を叩くと、反響がケツァールという鳥の鳴き声のように聞こえる。ケツァールは鮮やかな緑、赤、白の羽を持つ美しい鳥だ。マヤの人々はこれらの鳥が神々の使者だと信じていた。神殿の前で手を叩くと、神々が使者を通じてマヤの人々に語りかけているかのように聞こえるのだ。
16世紀にヨーロッパの探検家が初めてチチェン・イッツァに来る前のいつか、マヤの人々は街を去っていた。理由は確かではないが研究者は答えを探し続けている。現在はユネスコ世界遺産であり観光客に人気の目的地だ。しかし観光客による損傷のため建物は一般公開を閉鎖されている。これにより今後長年にわたって研究や鑑賞のために保護されることが期待されている。
sentencePairs: Construction of the city began around 1,500 years ago.
約1500年前に都市の建設が始まった。
The site had a large underground source of water.
大きな地下水源があった。
They designed a building to work as a calendar.
暦として機能する建物を設計した。
The echo sounds like the call of a bird called a quetzal.
反響がケツァールという鳥の鳴き声のように聞こえる。
The Maya people had left the city before European explorers arrived.
ヨーロッパの探検家が来る前にマヤの人々は街を去っていた。
The buildings have been closed due to damage caused by visitors.
観光客による損傷のため建物は閉鎖されている。

questions.question: What is one reason Chichén Itzá developed into a large city?
choices: The site was at the meeting point of two important trails.
The area had natural resources for farming and construction.
The weather in the region was constant throughout the year.
The first leader of the Maya people had been born there.
choiceAnalysis: 2つの重要な道の交差点→本文にない
農業と建設のための天然資源があった→正解。💡 good soil+plenty of stone=農業・建設の天然資源
天候が一定→本文にない
最初の指導者の出生地→本文にない

questions.question: The design of the Temple of Kukulcán possibly helped
choices: farmers living near Chichén Itzá to plan their work.
scientists to accurately record the movements of stars and planets.
the artists of Chichén Itzá to become the most famous in the Maya civilization.
the people living nearby to catch snakes for food.
choiceAnalysis: 近くの農民が作業を計画→正解。💡 help local farmers know when to plant their crops
科学者が星の動きを記録→不適切
芸術家が有名に→不適切
蛇を捕まえる→不適切

questions.question: Why did the Maya people believe that birds called quetzals were special?
choices: The birds built their nests on the steps of a temple.
The birds were thought to deliver messages from the gods.
The birds' calls sounded like the name of one of the gods.
The birds' feathers were needed for a ceremony at a temple.
choiceAnalysis: 鳥が神殿の階段に巣を作った→不適切
鳥が神々のメッセージを届けると考えられた→正解。💡 messengers of the gods=神々の使者
鳴き声が神の名前に似ている→不適切
羽が儀式に必要→不適切

questions.question: The buildings in Chichén Itzá were
choices: destroyed by European explorers who wanted to find gold and jewels.
damaged when people took stones from them to build hotels for visitors.
closed to keep them safe for future generations of tourists and researchers.
hidden for many years after a thick forest grew up around them.
choiceAnalysis: ヨーロッパ探検家が破壊→不適切
石を取って損傷→不適切
将来の世代のために閉鎖→正解。💡 closed+protect for many years to come+studied and enjoyed
森に隠れた→不適切

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true?
choices: Construction of Chichén Itzá began 1,500 years before the Maya people arrived.
People of the Maya civilization knew the number of days in a year.
There is no echo if a person claps in front of the Temple of Kukulcán.
Researchers have recently discovered why the Maya people left Chichén Itzá.
choiceAnalysis: マヤ人到着前に建設開始→マヤ人が建設した
マヤ文明の人々は1年の日数を知っていた→正解。💡 observed that one year lasted 365 days
拍手しても反響がない→反響がある
研究者が理由を発見→Nobody is sure

focusPoints.title: 比較・類似の表現
focusPoints.subtitle: Comparison & Similarity Patterns
focusPoints.explanation: P2Aでは動物の特徴を比較する表現が多用されている。similar to, just like, look similar toなど、類似性を表す構文を整理する。2級では比較構文の正確な理解が読解の鍵となる。
focusPoints.sourceQuote:
Tortoises look similar to turtles.
They are similar to kangaroos but smaller.
Just like kangaroos, wallabies stand on their back legs.

examples.en: Tortoises look similar to turtles. However, tortoises live on land.
examples.ja: リクガメはカメに似ているが、陸上で生活する。
examples.note: look similar to = ～に似て見える（外見の類似）

examples.en: Just like kangaroos, wallabies stand on their back legs.
examples.ja: カンガルーと同様に、ワラビーは後ろ足で立つ。
examples.note: Just like X, Y... = Xと同様にYは…（完全な類似）

examples.en: They are similar to kangaroos but smaller.
examples.ja: カンガルーに似ているが小さい。
examples.note: similar to A but B = Aに似ているがBという点で異なる（部分的類似）

practiceQuestions.q: 'is similar to' を別の比較表現で言い換えると？
practiceQuestions.a: is like / resembles / is comparable to / has something in common with

practiceQuestions.q: 'just as challenging as' はどういう構造か？
practiceQuestions.a: as + 形容詞 + as = 同等比較。just は強調（「まさに同じくらい」）。

practiceQuestions.q: 'The more frequently ... the more progress' はどんな構文？
practiceQuestions.a: the + 比較級, the + 比較級 = 「～すればするほど…」。

practicePassage.en: Learning a musical instrument is similar to learning a new language. Both activities require constant practice and patience. Playing the piano is just as challenging as playing the violin, though in different ways. The more frequently you practice, the more progress you will make. Interestingly, people who play music tend to perform better academically than those who do not.
practicePassage.ja: 楽器を学ぶことは新しい言語を学ぶのに似ている。どちらの活動も絶え間ない練習と忍耐を必要とする。ピアノを弾くことはバイオリンを弾くのと同じくらい難しいが、異なる方法で。練習の頻度が高いほど、より多くの進歩をする。興味深いことに、音楽を演奏する人はそうでない人よりも学業成績が良い傾向がある。

focusPoints.title: 提案のthat節と仮定法現在
focusPoints.subtitle: Suggest + Subjunctive Present
focusPoints.explanation: P3Aのメールで使われている 'I suggest that you divide...' は仮定法現在の重要構文。suggest/recommend/insist/propose などの後のthat節では動詞が原形になる（仮定法現在）。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: I suggest that you divide your students into five groups, and the members of each group can learn about the items in one of the rooms.

examples.en: I suggest that you divide your students into five groups.
examples.ja: 学生を5つのグループに分けることを提案します。
examples.note: suggest that S + 原形V（仮定法現在）: dividesではなくdivide

examples.en: The doctor recommended that she take the medicine twice a day.
examples.ja: 医師は彼女が1日2回薬を飲むよう勧めた。
examples.note: recommend that S + 原形V: takesではなくtake

examples.en: It is important that every student be on time.
examples.ja: すべての生徒が時間通りであることが重要だ。
examples.note: It is important that S + 原形V: isではなくbe

practiceQuestions.q: 'suggested that all students wear' のwearはなぜ原形？
practiceQuestions.a: 提案・要求のthat節では仮定法現在（動詞の原形）を使う。suggest/recommend/request that S (should) V。

practiceQuestions.q: 'It is essential that every member be given' のbe はなぜ？
practiceQuestions.a: It is essential/important/necessary that S + 動詞原形。形式主語構文でも仮定法現在を使う。

practiceQuestions.q: recommend that S (should) V を使って文を作ると？
practiceQuestions.a: The doctor recommended that she take the medicine three times a day.

highlightPatterns: I would like to have a meeting

practicePassage.en: The school committee suggested that all students wear uniforms starting next semester. Parents recommended that the school provide financial support for families who cannot afford the new uniforms. The principal requested that each teacher submit their opinion on the proposal by Friday. It is essential that every member of the school community be given a chance to voice their concerns.
practicePassage.ja: 学校委員会は来学期から全生徒が制服を着用するよう提案した。保護者は、新しい制服を買う余裕がない家庭に学校が財政支援を提供するよう勧めた。校長は各教師に金曜日までに提案についての意見を提出するよう求めた。学校コミュニティの全員が懸念を表明する機会を与えられることが不可欠だ。

focusPoints.title: 非人称受動態 It is believed / hoped that
focusPoints.subtitle: Impersonal Passive Construction
focusPoints.explanation: P3Bのチチェン・イッツァの文章で多用される 'It is believed that...' や 'It is hoped that...' は、主語を特定しない非人称受動態。学術的文章や報道英語でも頻出する重要構文。
focusPoints.sourceQuote:
It is believed that this temple was made so that a shadow on the temple in the shape of a snake's body joined up with statues...
It is hoped that doing this will protect these wonderful structures for many years to come.

examples.en: It is believed that this temple was made so that a shadow appears on special days.
examples.ja: 特別な日に影が現れるようにこの神殿が作られたと信じられている。
examples.note: It is believed that... = ...と信じられている（主語を明示しない）

examples.en: It is hoped that doing this will protect these wonderful structures.
examples.ja: これにより素晴らしい建造物が保護されることが期待されている。
examples.note: It is hoped that... = ...と期待されている（希望の非人称表現）

examples.en: Nobody is sure of the reason for this, but researchers are continuing to look for answers.
examples.ja: 理由は誰にもわからないが、研究者は答えを探し続けている。
examples.note: Nobody is sure = 誰も確信していない（否定の非人称表現）

practiceQuestions.q: 'It is believed that' を主語を変えて書き換えると？
practiceQuestions.a: Ancient Egyptians are believed to have been the first to build pyramids.

practiceQuestions.q: 'is thought to have been constructed' はどんな構造？
practiceQuestions.a: S + is thought to + have + 過去分詞。主文の時制より前の出来事を示す完了不定詞。

practiceQuestions.q: このパッセージの非人称受動態を全て挙げると？
practiceQuestions.a: It is believed, is thought to, It is estimated, are considered to, It is hoped

practicePassage.en: It is believed that ancient Egyptians were the first to build pyramids. The Great Pyramid of Giza is thought to have been constructed around 2560 BC. It is estimated that over two million stone blocks were used in its construction. The pyramids are considered to be among the greatest achievements of human engineering. It is hoped that modern technology will help researchers uncover more secrets about how they were built.
practicePassage.ja: 古代エジプト人が最初にピラミッドを建設したと信じられている。ギザの大ピラミッドは紀元前2560年頃に建設されたと考えられている。200万個以上の石のブロックが建設に使用されたと推定されている。ピラミッドは人類工学の最大の成果の一つと見なされている。現代の技術が、建設方法についてより多くの秘密を明らかにするのに役立つことが期待されている。

focusPoints.title: ディスコースマーカーと文章展開
focusPoints.subtitle: Discourse Markers & Text Organization
focusPoints.explanation: P2Bの芝ペンキの文章では、文章展開を示すディスコースマーカ（However, Still, Instead of, Furthermore, In the past, Nowadays）が効果的に使われている。文脈をつかむ鍵となる。
focusPoints.sourceQuote:
Lately, however, more homeowners have been trying a different approach.
Still, things have come a long way.
Furthermore, they spend the winter months sleeping.

examples.en: Lately, however, more homeowners have been trying a different approach.
examples.ja: しかし最近、多くの住宅所有者は違うアプローチを試している。
examples.note: however（逆接）: 前文との対比。文中に挿入される場合はカンマで囲む

examples.en: Still, things have come a long way.
examples.ja: それでも、状況は大きく改善した。
examples.note: Still（譲歩後の主張）: 前文の否定的内容にもかかわらず、と示す

examples.en: Furthermore, they spend the winter months sleeping.
examples.ja: さらに、冬の間は冬眠する。
examples.note: Furthermore（追加）: 前文に情報を追加する。Moreover / In additionと同義

practiceQuestions.q: このパッセージの論理構造を図式化すると？
practiceQuestions.a: 主張 → 根拠①(First of all) → 根拠②(Moreover) → 反論(However) → 具体例(For instance) → 再反論(Nevertheless) → 結論(In conclusion)
はるかに上回る。far は比較を強調する副詞。outweigh = ～より重い/重要である。
However = 一般的な逆接。Nevertheless = より強い逆接（前述の内容を認めた上で）。

focusPoints.highlightLabel: ディスコースマーカー

practicePassage.en: Renewable energy is becoming increasingly important worldwide. First of all, solar and wind power produce no harmful emissions. Moreover, the cost of installing solar panels has dropped significantly in recent years. However, renewable energy is not without its challenges. For instance, solar panels cannot generate electricity at night. Nevertheless, experts agree that the benefits of clean energy far outweigh the drawbacks. In conclusion, investing in renewable energy is essential for a sustainable future.
practicePassage.ja: 再生可能エネルギーは世界中でますます重要になっている。まず第一に、太陽光と風力は有害な排出物を出さない。さらに、ソーラーパネルの設置コストは近年大幅に下がっている。しかし、再生可能エネルギーには課題もある。例えば、ソーラーパネルは夜間に電気を生成できない。それにもかかわらず、クリーンエネルギーの利点は欠点をはるかに上回ると専門家は同意している。結論として、再生可能エネルギーへの投資は持続可能な未来に不可欠だ。

focusPoints.title: 今回の重要なパラフレーズ
focusPoints.subtitle: Key Paraphrases
focusPoints.explanation: 英検2級では本文の表現が設問で言い換えられる（パラフレーズ）。本文と選択肢の対応関係を見抜く力は合格の決め手となる。
focusPoints.sourceQuote:
①本文: they often have to take care of very large groups
→選択肢: They often must look after groups with many members.
②本文: I suggest that you divide your students into five groups
→選択肢: separate his students into several groups
③本文: good soil for growing crops, and plenty of stone for building
→選択肢: natural resources for farming and construction
④本文: Knowing such dates would help local farmers to know when to plant their crops
→選択肢: helped farmers living near Chichén Itzá to plan their work
⑤本文: The Maya people believed that these birds were the messengers of the gods
→選択肢: The birds were thought to deliver messages from the gods

examples.en: take care of very large groups → look after groups with many members
examples.ja: 大人数グループの世話をする → 多くのメンバーのグループの面倒を見る
examples.note: take care of = look after / very large = with many members

examples.en: divide students into five groups → separate students into several groups
examples.ja: 5つのグループに分ける → いくつかのグループに分ける
examples.note: divide = separate / five → several（具体→抽象）

examples.en: good soil for growing crops → natural resources for farming
examples.ja: 作物栽培に適した土壌 → 農業のための天然資源
examples.note: good soil = natural resources / growing crops = farming（具体→抽象）

practiceQuestions.a: close / cease operations / discontinue / stop running
monetary payment / economic support / severance pay
workers who were impacted / staff members who lost their jobs

practiceQuestions.q: 'negative impact on the community's economy' を言い換えると？
practiceQuestions.a: harmful effect on the local financial situation / damaging consequences for the area's businesses

practicePassage.en: The company decided to shut down its oldest factory. The facility had been operating for over fifty years. Workers were told that they would receive financial compensation. The company also promised to help affected employees find new positions. Local residents expressed concern that the closure would have a negative impact on the community's economy.
practicePassage.ja: 会社は最も古い工場を閉鎖する決定を下した。その施設は50年以上稼働していた。労働者には金銭的な補償が与えられると伝えられた。会社はまた、影響を受けた従業員が新しいポジションを見つけるのを支援すると約束した。地元住民は閉鎖が地域経済に悪影響を及ぼすことへの懸念を表明した。
